title: bridge_analyzer diagnostic report
description:
The machine-readable diagnostics contract (INV-16). Consumers: IDEs, CI, and the compiler's TypeScript half.

Additive-only until a major: a consumer MUST ignore fields it does not know, and a new optional field MUST NOT break one. Removing a field, renaming one, or changing its meaning bumps reportVersion — and a consumer that does not recognise the version MUST refuse to interpret the report rather than guess at it.

Output is canonical: keys sorted, no whitespace variance, no timestamps.

reportVersion.description: The version of this format. Not the analyzer's version.

summary.description: Severity counts, so a consumer need not tally them itself.

infos.description: Informational diagnostics — the compiler reporting a decision it made, so that it is never silent.

diagnostics.description: Every diagnostic, in a stable total order: file, line, column, code, message. The order is part of the contract — a CI job that diffs two reports must not see changes caused by traversal order.

location.description: A place in the analyzed project. Paths are project-relative, never absolute: an absolute path would make the report depend on where the project sits on disk.

length.description: Length in characters. Absent means a point.

code.description: The permanent code, e.g. BRG1201. Never reused, never renumbered.

category.description: Which part of the compiler owns it (Spec §9.2 code namespaces).

title.description: The code's invariant summary. Belongs to the code.

message.description: What is wrong here, specifically. Belongs to the occurrence.

docsSlug.description: The slug for `bridge_analyzer explain <code>` and the docs site.

location.description: Where. Absent for diagnostics about the project rather than a place in it.

hint.description: What the user should do next, as something they can act on.

related.description: Secondary locations that explain the diagnostic — e.g. the first of two conflicting declarations.

fixes.description: Machine-applicable edits. A fix is a span and the text to put there — nothing more expressive, because anything more expressive stops being mechanically applicable, which is the whole point of shipping them to an IDE.

description.description: What the fix does, in the user's terms.
